name: AZQ Stage 3 Wave B Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 3 Wave B so the Codex runner can evaluate the first Agenda command-layer work without hardcoding Stage 3 logic into the runner.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the patch.

baseline_checks.name: existing_regression_suite
baseline_checks.description: Verify the existing Stage 1 and Stage 2 regression suite still passes.

baseline_checks.name: agenda_storage_import
baseline_checks.description: Verify the Agenda storage facade still imports cleanly.

STAGE3-WAVEB-01.description: Implement azq agenda build as a thin command-layer writer.

checks.name: agenda_build_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the build module exposes a command-layer entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import build
assert any(hasattr(build, name) for name in ['build_agenda', 'build_tasks_for_deliverable', 'agenda_build']), 'missing Agenda build entrypoint'

checks.name: agenda_build_writes_canonical_task
checks.description: Verify the build path can validate a parent deliverable, allocate an id, and write one canonical task through shared Agenda storage helpers.
checks.code:
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.agenda import build
entry = getattr(build, 'build_agenda', None) or getattr(build, 'build_tasks_for_deliverable', None) or getattr(build, 'agenda_build', None)
assert entry is not None, 'build entrypoint not found'
written_tasks = []
parent = {'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Visible parent deliverable', 'artifact_description': 'Parent artifact description', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'drafted', 'created': '2026-03-18'}
with patch('azq.agenda.build.validate_parent_deliverable', return_value=parent), patch('azq.agenda.build.next_task_id', return_value='TASK_001'), patch('azq.agenda.build.write_task', side_effect=lambda record: written_tasks.append(dict(record))):
    entry('DELIV_001')
assert written_tasks, 'no task was written'
assert written_tasks[0]['task_id'] == 'TASK_001'
assert written_tasks[0]['deliverable_id'] == 'DELIV_001'
assert written_tasks[0]['description'], 'task description should not be blank'
assert written_tasks[0].get('created', '') != ''

human_checks: The build behavior stays intentionally small and deterministic rather than drifting into task execution, orchestration, or agent-loop behavior.
If the implementation uses a title field because the live Wave A schema still normalizes one, that is acceptable as long as the canonical description field remains populated and operator-visible.

STAGE3-WAVEB-02.description: Implement azq agenda list as a thin presentation layer.

checks.name: agenda_list_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the tasks module exposes a listing entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import tasks
assert any(hasattr(tasks, name) for name in ['list_tasks', 'show_tasks']), 'missing task listing entrypoint'

checks.name: agenda_list_reads_canonical_tasks
checks.description: Verify the list command prints canonical tasks and preserves the no-tasks case.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.agenda import tasks
entry = getattr(tasks, 'list_tasks', None) or getattr(tasks, 'show_tasks', None)
assert entry is not None, 'listing entrypoint not found'
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.agenda.tasks.load_all_tasks', return_value=[]):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry()
assert 'No tasks' in buf.getvalue()
buf = io.StringIO()
record = {'task_id': 'TASK_001', 'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'title': '', 'description': 'Visible task description', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'ready', 'execution_notes': '', 'created': '2026-03-18'}
with patch('azq.agenda.tasks.load_all_tasks', return_value=[record]):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry()
out = buf.getvalue()
assert 'TASK_001' in out
assert 'DELIV_001' in out
assert 'Visible task description' in out
assert 'ready' in out

human_checks: tasks.py stays presentation-only rather than becoming a second repository layer.
The terminal output is simple, readable, and centered on canonical task records rather than execution state changes.

STAGE3-WAVEB-03.description: Implement azq agenda show with exact-id task inspection.

checks.name: agenda_show_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the tasks module exposes an exact-id inspection entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import tasks
assert any(hasattr(tasks, name) for name in ['show_task', 'inspect_task']), 'missing task show entrypoint'

checks.name: agenda_show_loads_exact_task_and_prints_fields
checks.description: Verify the show command loads one canonical task by exact id and prints the key fields.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.agenda import tasks
entry = getattr(tasks, 'show_task', None) or getattr(tasks, 'inspect_task', None)
assert entry is not None, 'show entrypoint not found'
record = {'task_id': 'TASK_001', 'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'title': '', 'description': 'Inspect this task', 'dependencies': ['TASK_000'], 'status': 'ready', 'execution_notes': 'Visible note', 'created': '2026-03-18'}
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.agenda.tasks.load_task', return_value=record):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry('TASK_001')
out = buf.getvalue()
assert 'TASK_001' in out
assert 'DELIV_001' in out
assert 'Inspect this task' in out
assert 'TASK_000' in out
assert 'Visible note' in out

checks.name: agenda_show_missing_id_fails_cleanly
checks.description: Verify the show command handles a missing exact task id cleanly.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.agenda import tasks
entry = getattr(tasks, 'show_task', None) or getattr(tasks, 'inspect_task', None)
assert entry is not None, 'show entrypoint not found'
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.agenda.tasks.load_task', return_value=None):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry('TASK_999')
out = buf.getvalue()
assert 'not found' in out.lower()

human_checks: Exact-id lookup stays exact and does not drift into fuzzy search.
The inspection output stays task-focused and does not smuggle in start, complete, or log-writing behavior.

STAGE3-WAVEB-04.description: Implement azq agenda dag as a thin command-layer DAG refresher.

checks.name: agenda_dag_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the dags module exposes a DAG refresh entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import dags
assert any(hasattr(dags, name) for name in ['build_agenda_dag', 'agenda_dag', 'build_dag_for_deliverable']), 'missing agenda dag entrypoint'

checks.name: agenda_dag_builds_from_deliverable_tasks
checks.description: Verify the dag command validates the parent deliverable, loads tasks for that deliverable, and writes one canonical goal-level DAG artifact.
checks.code:
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.agenda import dags
entry = getattr(dags, 'build_agenda_dag', None) or getattr(dags, 'agenda_dag', None) or getattr(dags, 'build_dag_for_deliverable', None)
assert entry is not None, 'dag entrypoint not found'
written_dags = []
parent = {'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Parent deliverable', 'artifact_description': 'desc', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'drafted', 'created': '2026-03-18'}
tasks_for_deliverable = [
    {'task_id': 'TASK_001', 'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'description': 'First task', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'ready', 'execution_notes': '', 'created': '2026-03-18'},
    {'task_id': 'TASK_002', 'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'description': 'Second task', 'dependencies': ['TASK_001'], 'status': 'ready', 'execution_notes': '', 'created': '2026-03-18'}
]
with patch('azq.agenda.dags.validate_parent_deliverable', return_value=parent), patch('azq.agenda.dags.load_tasks_for_deliverable', return_value=tasks_for_deliverable), patch('azq.agenda.dags.write_dag', side_effect=lambda record: written_dags.append(dict(record))):
    entry('DELIV_001')
assert written_dags, 'no dag was written'
assert written_dags[0]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
assert 'DELIV_001' in written_dags[0]['deliverable_ids']
assert 'TASK_001' in written_dags[0]['task_ids']
assert 'TASK_002' in written_dags[0]['task_ids']

human_checks: The command remains deliverable-scoped at the CLI layer even though the canonical artifact written on disk is still goal-level.
Dependency-edge derivation should stay conservative and inspectable rather than inventing a planner or orchestration layer.

STAGE3-WAVEB-05.description: Wire Agenda commands into the CLI.

checks.name: agenda_cli_modules_exist
checks.description: Verify the Agenda CLI and router modules exist.

checks.name: agenda_cli_dispatch_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the Agenda CLI surface exposes a dispatch entrypoint through the router.
checks.code:
from azq.agenda import cli, router
assert hasattr(cli, 'dispatch'), 'missing agenda cli dispatch'
assert hasattr(router, 'dispatch'), 'missing agenda router dispatch'

checks.name: agenda_cli_branch_dispatches_to_modules
checks.description: Verify the Agenda CLI dispatch routes build, list, show, and dag to dedicated modules.
checks.code:
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.agenda import build as build_mod, cli, dags as dags_mod, tasks as tasks_mod
build_name = next(name for name in ['build_agenda', 'build_tasks_for_deliverable', 'agenda_build'] if hasattr(build_mod, name))
show_name = next(name for name in ['show_task', 'inspect_task'] if hasattr(tasks_mod, name))
dag_name = next(name for name in ['agenda_dag', 'build_agenda_dag', 'build_dag_for_deliverable'] if hasattr(dags_mod, name))
with patch.object(build_mod, build_name) as build_entry:
    assert cli.dispatch(['agenda', 'build', 'DELIV_001']) is True
    build_entry.assert_called_once_with('DELIV_001')
with patch.object(tasks_mod, 'list_tasks') as list_entry:
    assert cli.dispatch(['agenda', 'list']) is True
    list_entry.assert_called_once_with()
with patch.object(tasks_mod, show_name) as show_entry:
    assert cli.dispatch(['agenda', 'show', 'TASK_001']) is True
    show_entry.assert_called_once_with('TASK_001')
with patch.object(dags_mod, dag_name) as dag_entry:
    assert cli.dispatch(['agenda', 'dag', 'DELIV_001']) is True
    dag_entry.assert_called_once_with('DELIV_001')

checks.name: top_level_cli_routes_agenda_and_prints_help
checks.description: Verify the top-level CLI routes Agenda commands through the new router and prints Agenda help cleanly.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq import cli
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('sys.argv', ['azq', 'agenda']):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        cli.main()
out = buf.getvalue().lower()
assert 'agenda' in out
assert 'build' in out
assert 'list' in out
assert 'show' in out
assert 'dag' in out
with patch('azq.cli.dispatch_scintilla', return_value=False), patch('azq.cli.dispatch_finis', return_value=False), patch('azq.cli.dispatch_formam', return_value=False), patch('azq.cli.dispatch_agenda', return_value=True) as dispatch_agenda:
    with patch('sys.argv', ['azq', 'agenda', 'list']):
        cli.main()
dispatch_agenda.assert_called_once()

human_checks: The CLI wiring stays narrow and does not smuggle in task start, task complete, status, doctor, or task-runner behavior.
The first public Agenda loop feels consistent with the live router-based terminal command style rather than the older direct-branch CLI framing still present in some docs.
